title: Identification & Authentication Failures
summary: Take over the admin account through five different authentication weaknesses — no lockout, username enumeration, a predictable password-reset token, session fixation, and a forgeable "remember me" cookie — until a hardened login closes them all.
goal: You have your own low-privilege account (wiener:peter). Take over the admin account and read the flag in its console.
goalSecure: Try the earlier takeovers (brute force, enumeration, reset-token prediction, fixation, a forged cookie) — all should fail, while a genuine login still works.
rootCause: The login, session, reset, and persistence mechanisms each trust something the attacker controls or can predict — a weak password with no throttle, a chatty error, a guessable token, a client-set session id, an unsigned cookie — instead of a value only the legitimate server-and-user pair can produce.
lessons: Weak passwords plus no lockout/throttle make online brute force practical — rate-limit and forbid breached passwords.
Return one generic failure message (and equal timing) so login never reveals which usernames exist.
Password-reset tokens must be unpredictable (CSPRNG), single-use, and expiring — never sequential or timestamp-based.
Regenerate the session id at login and never accept one from the URL, or an attacker fixes a session the victim then authenticates.
Persistent-auth cookies must be signed/encrypted (or opaque handles) — an unsigned identity cookie is forgeable.
Verify passwords against a strong salted hash in constant time; trust only server-issued, integrity-protected credentials.

stages.title: Username enumeration
stages.defense: Hides the admin username; rejects unknown users.

stages.title: Predictable reset token
stages.defense: Strong admin password; reset via a token link.

stages.title: Session fixation
stages.defense: Real login; session id carried in a cookie.

stages.title: Forgeable "remember me" cookie
stages.defense: Persistent login via a "remember me" cookie.

stages.title: Verified, hardened authentication
stages.defense: Constant-time check, one generic error, lockout, random tokens, session rotation, signed cookie.
